user_query:
I'm on-call and need quick one-liner commands to check service connectivity by group. Please read input/services.csv and input/config.yaml, then:

1) Create output/commands.txt with one line per group listed in config.yaml (in that exact order). Each line should be a single command that checks all services in that group using host:port arguments in the same order they appear in the CSV for that group. Always include the flag "--timeout <N>" where N is the integer "timeout" value in config.yaml. Include the flag "--http" in that group's command if and only if at least one service in that group has protocol "http". Do not include --http for groups that are purely TCP.

2) Create output/plan.json summarizing, per group, the following fields: group, total (count of services), http_count, tcp_count, and targets (array of "host:port" strings in that group's input order). Only include groups present in config.yaml and ignore any services whose group is not listed there.

3) Create output/README.md with brief instructions describing the generated files and how to run the commands.

Do not perform any real network calls; just build the commands and summary based on the inputs.
